ref: That evening after sunset, all who were sick or had demons in them were brought to Jesus.
text: Li daar maŋ dajonyiɔk, ki niib din jii baatib nan binba ki narinbiit be bi ni na, ki baat namm Yisa boor ni.
translated: They went to the river, and there they saw a man sitting on a stone in the water, and he was called Jesus, the son of God.

ref: But if that one refuses to listen, take along one or two others. The Scriptures teach that every complaint must be proven true by two or more witnesses.
text: Ŋaan li-i tee ki u ki sak wun gbat turani, fan yiin niyenɔ amii niib bannlee ki pukin a paak ki yin saan u boor ni, a niib bannlee amii banntaa maŋ-n gbat barii maŋ ki teen a siara damm.
translated: The chief of the land and the people and the fetish priest and the soothsayer, all of them should come and gather at the entrance of the chief's compound, and the soothsayer and the elder should take the sheep and the goat and the chicken to the shrine.

ref: Other seeds fell on thin, rocky ground and quickly started growing because the soil wasn't very deep.
text: Ki leer mun din baa tanpuut paak siaminba ki tant ki yabi. Tant nba ki yab na, ki li pia ki kpaat yiama,
translated: The person who does not listen to advice will be left behind and will not be able to walk with their peers. If they do not walk with their peers, they will not know the right path to take.

ref: “Wonderful!” his master replied. “You are a good and faithful servant. I left you in charge of only a little, but now I will put you in charge of much more. Come and share in my happiness!”
text: Ki u yomdaanɔ na betɔ a ‘N faan, a tee daabiŋanɔe, ki bia tuun fanu; fa dia waaminna fanu biaŋinba na, li paak, n sa jii saamewa ki teen a nuu ni. Parpeenn nba ki n mɔk na, li ji tee min nan fin yare.’
translated: When you are going to your in-laws' house and you meet your father-in-law, you bow down to him, and you shake hands with him; if he is sitting on a mat, you squat, I stand up and greet him and bow down to him. An elderly person and I on the road, I stand up for him to pass.

ref: As soon as she touched them, her bleeding stopped, and she knew she was healed.
text: ŋaan set tan sii u liatir. Li taakpaak ni ki u faboŋ na ji din jit; ki u bann u gbanant ni nan u la laafia u yiaru na niwa.
translated: The chief has sent a message to the town. Everyone, old and young, should gather under the big tree; the chief and the elders will discuss the welfare of the town and the farm.
